perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Affiliates") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Representatives") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Affiliates' to 'Representatives' in the first sentence of section 1 creates confusion, because there is also an explicit definition of 'Affiliates'. This introduces ambiguity about the scope of the confidentiality obligation, directly contradicting the defined meaning of 'Affiliates'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Representatives") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process. Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the seine confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Patty, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Affiliates,' and the contradicted text uses this definition. By changing 'Affiliates' to 'Representatives' in the changed text, it creates an ambiguity and potential contradiction because 'Representatives' is used but 'Affiliates' has a defined meaning, creating a conflict based on misaligned terminology that also leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the seine confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Patty, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the same confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Signatory, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Party' to 'Signatory' creates confusion, because there is also definition of 'Parties'. This introduces ambiguity about the obligations in the section, directly contradicting the defined meaning of 'Parties'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Representatives") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process. Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the same confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Signatory, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Party' which aligns with the contradicted text that also uses 'Party' and defines it. The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Signatory', while the contradicted text relies on the definition of 'Party', leading to confusion and ambiguity about which entity the obligation applies to. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the term 'Party' is defined elsewhere in the contract and is now being used interchangeably with 'Signatory' without proper definition, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Inform on shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the above, Proprietary Data shall not include information (a) which is in or comes into the public domain or may be derived from information which is in or comes into the public domain through a source other than the Party receiving the information or its Affiliates; (b) is already in the possession of the Party receiving the information prior to receiving it from the other party; or (c) which becomes known to the party receiving the information through a source that it believes is not subject to a confidentiality agreed with or other obligation of secrecy to the other Party and such source is not subject to any other prohibition against transmitting the information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data' leads to an in-text contradiction because 'Proprietary Information' is defined and used extensively throughout the agreement. If 'Proprietary Data' is not equivalent, it's unclear what information is excluded under Section 2, and it is only reffering to a subset of what it can be considered Proprietary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Proprietary Data' creates an in-text contradiction. The original text defines and uses 'Proprietary Information' throughout the agreement, including the contradicted text which states that 'any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party... shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party'. By changing the term to 'Proprietary Data' in the changed text, it introduces ambiguity regarding what information is excluded from the definition of Proprietary Information, thus contradicting the contradicted text that grants exclusive property rights to the disclosing party over *all* Proprietary Information.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Proprietary Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that any Sensitive Information given by the disclosing Party to the other Party in connection with this Agreement shall be the sole and exclusive property of the disclosing Party, including all applicable rights to patents, copyrights, trademarks and trade secrets inherent therein and appurtenant thereto, and that no license or other right therein are created by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Sensitive Information' in Section 3 contradicts the original definition. The contract is predicated on protecting 'Proprietary Information,'. This creates a conflict as to what 'Sensitive Information' implies in the agreement, creating a doubt whether the agreement protects 'Sensitive Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Representatives") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process. Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the same confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Signatory, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Sensitive Information' directly contradicts the contradicted text, which explicitly refers to 'Proprietary Information' and outlines specific confidentiality obligations related to it. The contract defines 'Proprietary Information', but makes no mention of 'Sensitive Information'. Changing the original text to refer to something undefined makes the confidentiality obligations in the contradicted text unclear and unenforceable with respect to what is now termed 'Sensitive Information'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract now arguably uses two terms to mean the same thing without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands That the Proprietary Information disclosed to it is a unique and valuable asset of the other Party, that violation of this Agreement would cause the other Party immediate and irreparable harm, and that the remedies at law may be inadequate. Each Party therefore consents to the entry of a restraining order and a preliminary and permanent injunction restraining any such violation without proof of actual damages. Each Party agrees that any money damages shall include all pecuniary beets obtained by the receiving Party and its Affiliates as a result of any brunch of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands That the Trade Secrets disclosed to it is a unique and valuable asset of the other Party, that violation of this Agreement would cause the other Party immediate and irreparable harm, and that the remedies at law may be inadequate. Each Party therefore consents to the entry of a restraining order and a preliminary and permanent injunction restraining any such violation without proof of actual damages. Each Party agrees that any money damages shall include all pecuniary beets obtained by the receiving Party and its Affiliates as a result of any brunch of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Proprietary Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in Section 7 implies that only trade secrets are subject to the agreement's strongest protections, while the agreement is actually intended to protect more than just trade secrets. As the Proprietary Information is a term defined and extensively used, introducing the Trade Secrets, makes the text contradict what the definition means, creating issues on the enforcement, due to this misinterpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees to maintain and to cause its officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors, subsidiaries and affiliates (collectively "Representatives") to maintain the confidentiality of any Proprietary Information regarding the business affairs, property, or methods of operation or other Proprietary Information relating to the other and obtained by a Party during the term of-this Agreement. Bach Party hereto agrees that this Proprietary Information constitutes commercial and financial information and such Proprietary Information shall remain privileged and confidential and shall not be (a) used by the Party receiving it or its Affiliates for any purpose other than evaluating a proposed transaction (a "Transaction"), or (b) disclosed without the prior writes consent of the other Party except to the extort required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process. Each Party epees to keep confidential this Agreement and not to distribute or show copies of it or disclose the contents hereof to any person or entity not subject to this Agreement (except its Legal and accounting advisors with a written agreement to keep the same confidential) without the prior written consent of the other Signatory, except to the extent required by applicable law or judicial or administrative process.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Proprietary Information' in Section 7, while the changed text replaces this with 'Trade Secrets'. The contradicted text explicitly defines and uses 'Proprietary Information' throughout the agreement. By narrowing the scope from 'Proprietary Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in Section 7, the changed text contradicts the broader protection afforded to 'Proprietary Information' elsewhere in the agreement, creating a conflict as defined by Misaligned Terminology.
